description: Policy for mock/fake/stub usage in src/tests with explicit allowlist + waivers.

allowlist_entries.reason: Protocol test hooks use mock terminology for documented session-state probes, not incomplete production behavior.
allowlist_entries.revisit_condition: Revisit when session protocol test helpers are renamed or moved.

allowlist_entries.reason: Unix ancillary-data fixtures are documented kernel-boundary examples rather than runtime stubs.
allowlist_entries.revisit_condition: Revisit when ancillary-data conformance coverage is split into tests.

allowlist_entries.reason: Certificate planning text uses stub/mock words in proof descriptions, not fake execution paths.
allowlist_entries.revisit_condition: Revisit when plan certificate proof wording is normalized.

allowlist_entries.reason: Remote runtime examples include mock labels for deterministic peer fixtures.
allowlist_entries.revisit_condition: Revisit when remote peer fixtures are moved under tests.

allowlist_entries.reason: Obligation-table tests use mock labels for deterministic obligation records.
allowlist_entries.revisit_condition: Revisit when obligation fixtures are renamed away from mock terminology.

allowlist_entries.reason: Region-table tests use mock labels for deterministic region records.
allowlist_entries.revisit_condition: Revisit when region fixtures are renamed away from mock terminology.

allowlist_entries.reason: io_uring fallback wording is tracked by the reactor platform support work, not hidden fake behavior.
Test logging helper lives under src for crate reuse and only names test doubles.
allowlist_entries.revisit_condition: Revisit when shared test support moves out of runtime source.

allowlist_entries.reason: Transport mock export is separately cfg-gated by scripts/scan_stubs.sh.
allowlist_entries.revisit_condition: Revisit when transport mock module is retired or test-only.

allowlist_entries.reason: Transport tests use mock terminology for deterministic in-memory peers.
allowlist_entries.revisit_condition: Revisit when transport tests are moved under tests/.

allowlist_entries.reason: gRPC E2E coverage uses named mock services as intentional test doubles.
allowlist_entries.revisit_condition: Revisit when gRPC E2E doubles are renamed or replaced by fixture servers.

allowlist_entries.reason: HTTP shutdown coverage uses mock labels for deterministic server fixtures.
allowlist_entries.revisit_condition: Revisit when HTTP shutdown fixture naming is normalized.

allowlist_entries.reason: Property tests use synthetic region-operation fixtures and are not production placeholders.
allowlist_entries.revisit_condition: Revisit when property fixture names are normalized.

allowlist_entries.reason: Recovery-security tests use mock labels for adversarial fixtures.
allowlist_entries.revisit_condition: Revisit when recovery fixtures are renamed away from mock terminology.

allowlist_groups.reason: Normal tests and source-local test modules may use mock/fake/stub vocabulary for deterministic fixtures, adversarial peers, and fixture payloads. Conformance suites are classified before this rule and are not covered by this group.
allowlist_groups.revisit_condition: Re-audit during asupersync-u7y final ratchet signoff; new production or conformance paths must remain uncovered unless explicitly documented.

allowlist_groups.reason: Golden snapshots and checked-in artifact baselines are reference fixtures; mock/fake/stub words in those files describe fixture inputs or expected text rather than incomplete runtime behavior.
allowlist_groups.revisit_condition: Re-audit when fixture layout changes or when asupersync-oelvq2 aggregates final mock-code-finder evidence.

allowlist_groups.reason: Audit modules are tracked as evidence surfaces after source-truth repairs. The policy must report them separately from production stubs so stale prose is reviewed during final signoff instead of mixed into runtime-code failures.
allowlist_groups.revisit_condition: Re-audit all matching paths during asupersync-u7y final gap closure and keep the generated JSONL evidence attached through asupersync-oelvq2.

allowlist_groups.reason: These exact paths are the known conformance lexical hits after the source-truth repair beads closed. They remain visible as conformance-placeholder coverage rows, while any new conformance file or helper with mock/fake/stub/placeholder/todo/unimplemented text fails until explicitly documented.
allowlist_groups.revisit_condition: Re-audit exact path list during asupersync-u7y final gap closure and split or remove entries whose conformance claim has been rewritten without placeholder language.

allowlist_groups.reason: These exact runtime-core production paths are known lexical hits whose behavior is either live, cfg/test-support bounded, or tracked by closed mock-code-finder repair beads. New production source paths remain uncovered and fail the gate.
allowlist_groups.revisit_condition: Re-audit exact path list during asupersync-u7y final ratchet signoff and retire entries as wording or test-support boundaries are normalized.

allowlist_groups.reason: These exact gRPC paths are known lexical hits after gRPC compression, status, and deadline source-truth repairs landed. Future gRPC production placeholders remain policy violations.
allowlist_groups.revisit_condition: Re-audit after asupersync-oelvq2 aggregate proof and asupersync-u7y final gap closure.

allowlist_groups.reason: These exact messaging paths are known lexical hits in live or tracked transport surfaces. Kafka phase-0 stubs stay under expiring waivers; new messaging paths remain uncovered.
allowlist_groups.revisit_condition: Re-audit after asupersync-oelvq2 aggregate proof and retire entries as transport wording is normalized.

allowlist_groups.reason: These exact observability production paths are known lexical hits after the OTLP/metrics repair beads closed. Audit modules are separately categorized as stale audit prose.
allowlist_groups.revisit_condition: Re-audit after observability evidence aggregation in asupersync-oelvq2 and final gap closure in asupersync-u7y.

allowlist_groups.reason: The browser reactor path contains known platform-boundary fallback terminology and remains separated from default native runtime behavior; macOS and Windows fallback exports remain expiring waivers.
These exact TLS paths are known lexical hits in live connector/error handling surfaces rather than hidden fake TLS behavior.
allowlist_groups.revisit_condition: Re-audit during asupersync-u7y final ratchet signoff.

allowlist_groups.reason: These exact web-runtime paths are known lexical hits in live middleware/response code or documented test-support hooks. New web production placeholders remain uncovered.
allowlist_groups.revisit_condition: Re-audit during asupersync-u7y final ratchet signoff.

waivers.reason: Phase-0 Kafka API surface intentionally uses stub implementations until real transport completion.
Phase-0 consumer path is intentionally stubbed; tracked for replacement in coverage closure.
The uring module is a documented stub gate and is intentionally tracked as technical debt.
Platform fallback/stub export remains while deterministic cross-platform support is hardened.
Platform fallback/stub export remains while deterministic cross-platform support is hardened.
